title: GlassWorm malware hits 400+ code repos on GitHub, npm, VSCode, OpenVSX
summary: Threat actors are distributing the new GlassWorm malware through backdoored packages across multiple popular code repositories. This campaign highlights the growing risk of supply chain attacks targeting developer ecosystems.

title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw (CVE-2026-32746) Enables Unauthenticated Root RCE via Port 23
summary: A newly discovered zero-day vulnerability in Telnetd allows remote attackers to execute root commands without authentication. Administrators are urged to disable Telnet services immediately until a patch is released.

title: An AI-powered phishing campaign has compromised hundreds of organizations
summary: Cybercriminals are leveraging generative AI to craft highly personalized and convincing phishing emails at scale. The campaign has successfully bypassed traditional email security gateways across global enterprises.

title: Telnyx package latest hit in PyPI supply-chain compromise
summary: A malicious version of the Telnyx Python package was briefly available on PyPI, attempting to exfiltrate developer credentials. This marks the latest incident in an ongoing wave of targeted software supply chain attacks.

title: 15-Year-Old strongSwan Flaw Lets Attackers Crash VPNs via Integer Underflow
summary: Security researchers have uncovered a long-standing vulnerability in the strongSwan IPsec implementation that can lead to remote denial of service. The integer underflow bug affects numerous enterprise VPN appliances running the software.

title: Hacker walks away with $24.5 million after breaching Resolv DeFi platform
summary: A sophisticated smart contract exploit allowed a lone attacker to drain millions from the Resolv decentralized finance platform. The incident underscores the persistent security challenges within the cryptocurrency and DeFi sectors.
